Name: Add Tag to an Attribute
Author: admin
Description: Add tags to attributes in MISP.

DynamicResults.ResultExample: [{"name": "Global tag unique___test(7) successfully attached to Attribute(9).", "message": "Global tag unique___test(7) successfully attached to Attribute(9).", "url": "/tags/attachTagToObject"}]

Parameters.Description: Specify a comma-separated list of attribute identifiers to which you want to add tags. Note: If both “Attribute Name“ and “Attribute UUID“ are specified, action will work with “Attribute UUID“ values.
Specify the ID or UUID of the event, where to search for attributes. This parameter is required, if “Attribute Search“ is set to “Provided Event“ or Object UUID is provided.
Specify a comma-separated list of tags that you want to add to attributes.

Specify a comma-separated list of categories. If specified, action will only add tags to attributes that have matching category. If nothing is specified, action will ignore categories in attributes. Possible values: External Analysis, Payload Delivery, Artifacts Dropped, Payload Installation.

Specify a comma-separated list of attribute types. If specified, action will only add tags to attributes that have matching attribute type. If nothing is specified, action will ignore types in attributes. Example values: md5, sha1, ip-src, ip-dst
Specify the uuid of the object that contains the desired attribute.
Specify a comma-separated list of attribute UUIDs to which you want to add new tags. Note: If both “Attribute Name“ and “Attribute UUID“ are specified, action will work with “Attribute UUID“ values.

Specify, where action should search for attributes. If “Provided Event“ is selected, action will only search for attributes or attribute UUIDs in event with ID/UUID provided in “Event ID“ parameter. If “All Events“, action will search for attributes among all events and add sighting for all attributes that match our criteria.

AIDescription:
Adds tags to attributes in MISP. This action allows users to apply one or more tags to specific attributes identified by name or UUID. The search for attributes can be restricted to a specific MISP Event, a specific Object within an event, or performed across all events. Users can further filter the target attributes by category and type to ensure tags are applied precisely. 

### Parameters
| Parameter | Type | Mandatory | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Tag Name | String | Yes | A comma-separated list of tags to add to the attributes. |
| Attribute Name | String | No | A comma-separated list of attribute values to tag. |
| Attribute UUID | String | No | A comma-separated list of attribute UUIDs to tag. If both Name and UUID are provided, UUID takes precedence. |
| Event ID | String | No | The ID or UUID of the MISP event. Required if 'Attribute Search' is set to 'Provided Event' or if an 'Object UUID' is specified. |
| Attribute Search | Dropdown | No | Determines the search scope: 'Provided Event' (default) or 'All Events'. |
| Object UUID | String | No | The UUID of a specific object within an event to narrow down the attribute search. |
| Category | String | No | Comma-separated list of MISP categories (e.g., 'Payload delivery', 'External analysis') to filter attributes. |
| Type | String | No | Comma-separated list of MISP attribute types (e.g., 'md5', 'ip-src') to filter attributes. |

### Additional Notes
- If 'Attribute Search' is set to 'Provided Event', the 'Event ID' parameter must be populated.
- If an 'Object UUID' is provided, the 'Event ID' must also be provided.

### Flow Description
1. **Initialization**: The action initializes the MISP manager using the provided API credentials and validates the input parameters.
2. **Tag Verification**: It checks the MISP instance to ensure the requested tags exist; if none of the tags exist, the action fails.
3. **Attribute Discovery**: It searches for attributes based on the provided identifiers (Name/UUID) and filters (Category/Type) within the chosen scope (Event/Object/Global).
4. **Tag Application**: For every attribute found, the action sends a request to MISP to attach each of the valid tags.
5. **Result Reporting**: The action returns a summary of successfully tagged attributes and lists any tags or attributes that were not found.
